page: Amazon Web Services c8gd.large is a compute-optimized instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 2 dedicated physical cores on the ARM64 architecture, utilizing the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.8 GHz. The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It includes 118 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-threaded performance in specific workloads, with gzip compression reaching 30.31 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.16 GB/sec. However, multi-core and multi-threaded performance is weaker, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 3402.79 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 3236.0. Memory bandwidth is moderate for large block reads at 55.37 GB/sec but drops to weaker tiers for write operations. Cryptographic and complex vector operations are limited due to the absence of AVX and AMX instruction sets, resulting in lower Passmark CPU encryption and extended instruction scores.

The c8gd.large offers a cost-efficient entry point for ARM64-based compute workloads that require local high-speed NVMe storage. Given the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and the dedicated core allocation, it is suited for single-threaded processing tasks, lightweight database operations, and basic web serving. It is less suitable for memory-intensive applications, heavy multi-threaded compilation, or machine learning inference due to its constrained memory capacity and lower multi-core benchmark results.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors running at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 4.0 GB of system memory and includes 118 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show strong single-threaded capabilities, particularly in compression and decompression tasks, though multi-core and memory write speeds are relatively weak. The instance lacks AVX and AMX instruction sets, limiting its efficiency in advanced vector and cryptographic operations. With its balanced compute-to-memory ratio and fast local storage, the c8gd.large is suited for lightweight database operations, single-threaded processing, and basic web serving workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.large is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 2 Graviton cores, 4.0 GB RAM, and 118 GB local NVMe SSD storage, suited for lightweight database and single-threaded tasks.

tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated physical cores and fast local NVMe SSD storage for single-threaded workloads.

Equipped with 4.0 GB of memory and 118 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-threaded compression and decompression performance
Built on the Nitro hypervisor with 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
